success_surface: Correct transposition of wide-format tabular data into long-format with proper range constraints
effective_behaviors: Dynamically detect header row position instead of assuming fixed row indices
Programmatically inspect source and target sheet structures before manipulation
Respect explicitly specified cell range boundaries during write operations
Clear target range before populating with new data to avoid residual content
Preserve native Excel data types (datetime, numeric) rather than forcing string conversion
Include zero-valued entries in transposition when format requires full data coverage
Verify output by reading back modified cells after save operation
Save to new output path rather than overwriting the original file
evidence: Agent searched for 'ITEM' cell value to find header row at row 6 instead of assuming row 1
Agent listed wb.sheetnames and sampled both Data and Transpose sheets before manipulation
Agent respected answer_position 'Transpose'!A3:C970 by limiting writes to max 970 rows
Agent cleared target range rows 3-970 with None values before writing transposed data
Agent preserved datetime.datetime objects in Date column instead of converting to strings
Agent included qty=0 values in output matching the expected transpose format pattern
Agent verified output by iterating through first 10 rows after save to confirm values
Agent saved to /mnt/data/outputs/...output.xlsx leaving original init.xlsx untouched

memory_items.title: Dynamic Header Location
memory_items.lesson: Search for known header text values to locate the actual data start row rather than assuming fixed positions
memory_items.generalization: When manipulating spreadsheets, iterate through rows searching for specific column header text (e.g., 'ITEM', 'Date', etc.) to dynamically identify the data boundary instead of hardcoding row indices

memory_items.title: Range-Bounded Write Operations
memory_items.lesson: Always respect the specified target cell range and clear it before writing new data
memory_items.generalization: Before writing to a target range, verify the answer_position constraint, clear existing content within those bounds using iterative cell clearing, then limit write loops to not exceed the maximum allowed row/column

memory_items.title: Native Type Preservation
memory_items.lesson: Maintain datetime and numeric types as-is without coercing to strings
memory_items.generalization: When writing cell values, preserve Python datetime objects and numeric types directly; only convert to strings when absolutely necessary for compatibility or display purposes

memory_items.title: Post-Write Verification
memory_items.lesson: Read back and log sample output cells after saving to confirm correctness
memory_items.generalization: After calling save(), re-open or read the saved file and print a verification sample of the affected cells to catch errors before final response

edits.content:
## 6. Trace-Derived Lessons

**Dynamic Header Detection**:
- Search for known header text values (e.g., 'ITEM', 'Date', 'QTY') to locate the actual data start row rather than assuming fixed row indices
- Iterate through rows checking column 1 until header text is found
- This handles files with empty rows, intro text, or inconsistent formatting

**Range-Bounded Write Operations**:
- Before writing to a target range, verify the answer_position constraint (e.g., 'Sheet'!A1:Z100)
- Clear existing content within those bounds using iterative cell setting to None
- Limit write loops with conditionals (e.g., `if target_row <= max_allowed`) to prevent overflow

**Native Type Preservation**:
- Maintain datetime objects, integers, floats, and booleans without coercing to strings
- Only convert to strings when explicit text formatting is required
- Validate data types after processing to ensure Excel recognizes them correctly

**Post-Write Verification**:
- After calling save(), read back the saved file or access the workbook object
- Print a verification sample (first 5-10 rows) of affected cells
- Report dimension counts (rows written, columns affected) in the final summary

**Full Data Coverage**:
- Include zero-value entries when the target format expects complete data coverage
- Do not skip null/zero values unless explicitly instructed to filter them
